title: Lithocardium thibaudianum
intro: Lithocardium thibaudianum, also known as Digitalis thibaudiana, is a plant species within the foxglove family (Apocynaceae) that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including the treatment of cardiac conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, due to its ability to increase the force of heart contractions and improve cardiac output. The bioactive constituents of Lithocardium thibaudianum include cardenolides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Lithocardium thibaudianum has been used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat cardiac conditions under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
taxonomy: Lithocardium thibaudianum is classified within the family Acanthaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus family. The Acanthaceae family is part of the order Lamiales in the larger angiosperm clade. The Lamiales order comprises plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers and four- or five-merous corollas. This classification places Lithocardium thibaudianum within the broader context of flowering plant evolution.

preparations: Lithocardium thibaudianum is a plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of Lithocardium thibaudianum are believed to have diuretic and cardiotonic properties. The plant's extracts are used to treat conditions such as high blood pressure and heart failure. In herbal medicine, Lithocardium thibaudianum is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
